saints.name: Repose of Saint Raphael, Bishop of Brooklyn

troparion.text: Your proclamation has gone forth throughout North America, / calling the scattered sheep into the unity of the church. / hearing your voice, they responded to your teaching, / and through your writings you instructed them in piety. / Now guided by your example, O Father Raphael, / we sing hymns of praise unto Christ our God. / Glory to Him Who gave you strength! / Glory to Him Who granted you a crown! / Glory to Him Who, through you, grants healing to all!

kontakion.text: You were a guardian and a defender of the Church’s teaching; / you protected your flock from false doctrines and confirmed them in the true faith. / O holy father Raphael, son of Syria and glory of North America, / always intercede before the Lord that our souls may be saved.

saints.name: Venerable Prokopios the Confessor of Decapolis

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “O citizen of the desert...”)
Having advanced in asceticism, O Father Prokopios,  / you went from strength to strength.  / By venerating the icon of Christ, you were shown as one comparable to the Martyrs.  / With them, intercede for those who cry to you:  / “Glory to Him Who has strengthened you.  / Glory to Him Who has crowned you. /  Glory to Him, Who through you, works healings for all.”

kontakion.text:
(Special Melody: “You appeared today...”)
O most glorious Prokopios, /  initiate of heavenly Mysteries, /  the Church honors you today as a morning star which dispels all the gloom of heresy.

saints.name: Venerable Titus the Presbyter of the Kiev Near Caves

troparion.text: In the love of the living brethren,  / you beheld the Hater of good1 who defeated you, turning you toward enmity, /  but knowing your previous works, the Lord, the fountain of love,  / turned you once more toward brotherly love, in which you lived, O God pleaser.  / Pray for us, that we may live in brotherly love.

kontakion.text: O Titus, you emulated the Lord's humility, /  for as a priest you humbled yourself before the proud Deacon Evagrius without malice; /  therefore, you not only received the continuance of your earthly life from the Angel who healed you,  / but also the enjoyment of eternal life;  / as you rejoice in it, deliver us from harsh anger and vindictiveness.

saints.name: Venerable Titus the Soldier, of the Kiev Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Titus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: Venerable Thalelaios the Hermit of Syria

troparion.text: O Thallélaios, you conquered idolatrous error by your martyric contest. /  You are a treasury of healing, freely curing all who run to your church with faith, and who cry out with love: “Glory to Him Who strengthened you. /  Glory to Him Who made you wondrous.  / Glory to Him Who, through you, works healings for all.”

kontakion.text: You were a fellow contestant with the martyrs, O Thallélaios,  /  and you became a soldier of the King of Glory,  /  trampling upon the pride of the idolaters by your trials and torments.  /  Therefore, we praise your memory.

saints.name: Saints Asclepius and James of Syria

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy fathers Asclepius and James, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: Martyrs Julian, Eunos, Beza, and Mekaro of Alexandria

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Leander, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.
